perspectives.headline: Taylor Farms highlighted an FDA mistake. PR veterans say it muddied the message.

The JPMorgan boss says it isn't arrogance that ruins leaders, it's insecurity. And the warning signs are flattery, friendly hires, and glossy PowerPoints.…

perspectives.headline: Jamie Dimon says markets underestimate risks and he wouldn't buy stocks or Treasurys at current prices
perspectives.excerpt: JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon said investors are underestimating the risks facing the global economy and that he wouldn't buy either equities or long-dated U.S. Treasurys at their current prices. In an hourlong interview with Wilfred Frost released late Monday, Dimon said markets aren't fully accounting for a growing list of geopolitical and fiscal threats.…

stories.headline_en: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved

perspectives.headline: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
perspectives.excerpt: Anthropic can finally start cutting checks to a group of authors and book publishers that sued the AI lab over copyright infringement. A federal judge gave final approval Monday of Anthropic’s landmark $1.5 billion settlement of a class action copyright lawsuit, Reuters reported. Judge William Alsup of the U.S.…

perspectives.headline: Anthropic's record $1.5 billion copyright settlement with authors was just approved
perspectives.excerpt: Anthropic's $1.5 billion settlement of a class action copyright lawsuit brought by authors and publishers received final court approval on Monday. U.S. District Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin, sitting in the Northern District of California, signed off on the agreement, which is believed to be the largest settlement in the history of U.S. copyright law, according to Reuters.

perspectives.headline: Judge approves Anthropic's record-breaking $1.5 billion settlement for AI copyright lawsuit

stories.headline_en: Trump’s proposed ban on Kimi and other Chinese AI models could strengthen Beijing’s hand

perspectives.headline: Trump’s proposed ban on Kimi and other Chinese AI models could strengthen Beijing’s hand
perspectives.excerpt: The Trump administration has had plenty of targets in its sights in recent months. The newest are China’s cutting-edge AI models. Following the release of Kimi K3 , the latest model from Moonshot AI, the White House is reportedly considering options that could effectively ban models like it, along with Alibaba’s Qwen model, the latest version of which was released just days after K3.…

perspectives.headline: The secret Trump administration battle to fight Chinese AI
perspectives.excerpt: The Trump administration is showing signs it could ban cutting-edge Chinese AI models — a momentous move that could lock in dominance by OpenAI and Anthropic. Why it matters: Parts of the administration have tried to implement de facto bans on foreign open-source models before, knowledgeable sources tell Axios. Last week's rise of Chinese model Kimi is reigniting those efforts.…

perspectives.headline: What China's internet is saying about Moonshot's hot new Kimi model
perspectives.excerpt: Moonshot AI's latest Kimi K3 model is giving China's internet plenty to talk about. The Chinese startup unveiled the open-weight model on Thursday, touting its ability to compete with leading systems from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. Days later, Moonshot AI temporarily suspended new subscriptions, citing overwhelming demand that stretched its computing capacity.…

perspectives.excerpt: Protesters and police clashed in Bologna, Italy on Monday after Moroccan-born entrepreneur Abderrahim Fakir died while being forcibly restrained by police. Widely shared footage of Fakir's death show police holding the 42-year-old face-down on the ground, where he repeatedly calls for help before falling still.

perspectives.headline: 'A lot of aspirations, a lot of priorities, but already criticism' for new UK government
perspectives.excerpt: The UK has a new prime minister and a new government that seem eager to get started on its promise to change the path the country is on. Ahead of a first cabinet meeting, a cut in the VAT on energy bills has been announced, as cost of living as come out as one of the government's main priorities. But the announcement is already being criticised. FRANCE 24's Benedicte Paviot tells us more.

Fans packed the streets, waving Spanish flags and cheering as the players travelled from near Moncloa Palace to Cibeles Square after Sunday's 1-0 victory over Argentina in the World Cup final.
